skill.name: fiftyone-embeddings-visualization
skill.description: Guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through FiftyOne embedding visualization workflows using UMAP, t-SNE, or PCA. It helps users compute embeddings, open the FiftyOne App, inspect clusters, find outliers, and compare labels or predictions.
skill.summary: Create and inspect 2D FiftyOne embedding visualizations for image datasets.
skill.author: AdonaiVera

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk alerts, but most are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, prose, and documentation URLs in SKILL.md. The confirmed concern is moderate operational risk because the skill guides users to download a FiftyOne plugin and optionally install a Python dependency. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential access, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or covert command execution.

medium_findings.title: External Plugin and Dependency Installation Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to download and enable the @voxel51/brain plugin and suggests asking before running pip install umap-learn. This is legitimate for FiftyOne visualization, but it creates supply-chain and environment-change risk if users approve unreviewed installs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly describe downloading a FiftyOne plugin and optionally installing a Python package. The behavior matches the skill purpose, but it still changes the user environment.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Formatting Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick execution findings. Review shows these are Markdown code fences and inline code examples for FiftyOne MCP tool calls, not executable Ruby or shell backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside SKILL.md documentation blocks and tables. No executable source file or Ruby command substitution was found in the reviewed skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Localhost URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the local FiftyOne App and official FiftyOne or GitHub documentation resources. They do not show credential exfiltration or covert network communication in the skill text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are visible user-facing references to localhost and documentation. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code or host enumeration. They appear to match ordinary prose, table labels, or UI instructions in the Markdown skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain description text, a table header, t-SNE guidance, and UI navigation steps. No hash function, cipher implementation, or reconnaissance command is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Plugin and Dependency Installation Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to download and enable the @voxel51/brain plugin and suggests asking before running pip install umap-learn. This is legitimate for FiftyOne visualization, but it creates supply-chain and environment-change risk if users approve unreviewed installs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly describe downloading a FiftyOne plugin and optionally installing a Python package. The behavior matches the skill purpose, but it still changes the user environment.

content.value_statement: Image datasets can hide clusters, outliers, and label problems that are difficult to inspect in tables. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through FiftyOne embedding workflows that make dataset structure visible.
actual_capabilities: Sets the active FiftyOne dataset context before running visualization steps.
Guides users to launch the FiftyOne App for delegated Brain operators.
Checks for the @voxel51/brain plugin and shows how to enable it.
Computes image embeddings with supported FiftyOne model zoo models.
Creates 2D visualizations using UMAP, t-SNE, or PCA.
Explains how to inspect clusters, outliers, and class separation in the Embeddings panel.
limitations: Requires a running FiftyOne MCP server and a loaded image dataset.
Requires the @voxel51/brain plugin for the documented Brain operators.
Large datasets can take significant compute time and memory.
The skill provides workflow guidance and does not replace statistical validation.

use_cases.title: Explore Image Dataset Structure
use_cases.description: Create a 2D embedding map to reveal natural groups and dataset coverage.

use_cases.title: Find Outliers Before Training
use_cases.description: Use uniqueness scores and embedding position to identify unusual or mislabeled samples.

use_cases.title: Compare Labels and Predictions
use_cases.description: Color embedding points by ground truth or predictions to inspect class separation and error patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Embedding Map
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for an existing FiftyOne dataset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the FiftyOne embeddings visualization skill on my current dataset. Check for existing embeddings, create a 2D UMAP view, and tell me how to open it in the App.

prompt_templates.title: Compute Embeddings First
prompt_templates.scenario: Dataset does not have embedding fields yet.
prompt_templates.prompt: My FiftyOne dataset has no embeddings. Use CLIP embeddings, compute similarity, then create a 2D visualization named dataset_overview.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Outliers
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my dataset for outliers with FiftyOne Brain. Compute uniqueness, create an embedding visualization, and show me how to review the most unusual samples.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Behavior
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of labels and predictions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FiftyOne embedding visualization for prediction analysis. Use existing embeddings if available, then guide me to compare ground truth and prediction clusters in the App.

output_examples.input: Visualize my image dataset with UMAP.
output_examples.output: The assistant sets the dataset context, checks Brain operators, computes or reuses embeddings, creates a UMAP visualization, and directs you to the Embeddings panel.

output_examples.input: Find strange samples in this dataset.
output_examples.output: The assistant computes uniqueness scores, creates a visualization, and explains how to inspect isolated points and sort by uniqueness.

output_examples.input: Compare class separation for my predictions.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates an embedding map and guides you to color points by labels and predictions to find overlap or misclassification patterns.

best_practices: Confirm the active dataset and Brain plugin status before computing embeddings.
Start with UMAP for exploration, then use t-SNE only when local structure matters more.
Run on a subset first when datasets are large or compute resources are limited.
anti_patterns: Do not install plugins or packages without user approval.
Do not assume an embeddings field exists before checking the operator schema.
Do not treat visual clusters as proof without reviewing the underlying samples.

faq.question: Does this skill require FiftyOne?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires a FiftyOne MCP server, a loaded dataset, and the FiftyOne App for delegated Brain operators.

faq.question: Can it compute embeddings from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to use FiftyOne Brain with supported model zoo models such as CLIP, DINOv2, ResNet, or MobileNet.

faq.question: Which visualization methods are supported?
faq.answer: The documented workflow supports UMAP, t-SNE, and PCA through FiftyOne Brain visualization operators.

faq.question: Does it send my data to an external service?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the skill text. It does reference plugin downloads and documentation, so users should review install actions.

faq.question: Can it help find mislabeled samples?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide outlier review and class comparison, but a human should verify each suspected label issue.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning for plugin download and package installation guidance.
